title: C Values, Integer Conversions, and Size Arithmetic · C Contract Brief: Checked Size Arithmetic
summary: A binary record parser receives attacker-controlled counts that cross signedness and multiplication boundaries before allocation. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem checked size arithmetic increment in checked size and conversion policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Reject overflowing addition, multiplication, alignment, and growth calculations before allocation using portable checked patterns or C23 checked arithmetic.
Use size_t for object sizes, ptrdiff_t for valid pointer differences, and guarded conversions at API boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Checked Size Arithmetic · Theory Checked Size Arithmetic
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 5968. Produce bounded C evidence to reject a size calculation that exceeds its bound; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Reject overflowing addition, multiplication, alignment, and growth calculations before allocation using portable checked patterns or C23 checked arithmetic.
